mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate keeps real financing costs compressed and supports risk asset valuations, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days as investors price a delayed exit from accommodation. This signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS stays near the lower bound but the 10-year yield falls in at least 60% of 20-day windows during similar low-rate episodes, or if CPIAUCSL acceleration dominates and reverses the sign consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a short span, the market interprets weakening labor demand as a growth scare, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days through lower expected future rates and lower inflation pressure.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-deterioration episodes do not precede lower 10-year yields over 20 trading days, or if the yield rises in a majority of such cases despite weaker payrolls and higher unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous upswing in RSAFS and HOUST indicates demand and construction activity are firming together, which lifts growth expectations and steepens rate expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST strength does not improve the sign hit rate versus either series alone, or if 20-day yield changes are negative in most periods when both series trend up together.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO alongside flat or weak PAYEMS signals production stress without broad labor absorption, which depresses expected real activity and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if below-average INDPRO does not coincide with lower forward yields, or if payroll growth offsets the industrial decline and the sign flips to positive in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPIAUCSL inflation combined with still-very-low FEDFUNDS implies policy is behind the curve, so long rates reprice upward as the market anticipates future tightening and higher term premia.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by more than 1.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise episodes with low policy rates do not produce positive 5-day or 20-day yield responses, or if yield declines occur as often as increases after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A renewed rise in GDPC1 after prior softness indicates growth reacceleration, which lifts expected real rates and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the following quarter rather than the next few days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 momentum fails to coincide with higher 60-day yields in repeated quarters, or if the relationship is systematically inverted during rebound periods.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor-market slack is increasing faster than output can absorb it, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as recession odds rise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 0 and UNRATE rises above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined slack trigger does not outperform single-series signals, or if triggered episodes do not produce lower 20-day yields more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth with rising housing starts indicates a synchronized demand upswing that lifts inflation and real growth expectations, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days in expansionary periods.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise above their prior 3-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint demand strength does not produce positive short-horizon yield responses, or if the sign is negative in a majority of synchronized upturns across walk-forward samples.
